officialSources.title: Find out what's changed in a file
evidence: Google documents version history for Docs, Sheets, Slides and Vids.
Google documents named versions, previous-version restoration, cell edit history, and current-editor color attribution.

officialSources.title: Collaborate in Sheets
evidence: Google documents viewing and restoring older versions in Sheets.
Google documents comments, task assignment, Activity dashboard access, and Sheets email notifications.

officialSources.title: Manage data in Sheets
evidence: Google documents version history access in Sheets from the Manage data help page.
Google documents the Activity stream as a way to see changes in Sheets.

officialSources.title: View the activity on your Google Docs, Sheets and Slides
evidence: Google documents an Activity dashboard for Docs, Sheets and Slides.
Google documents viewer and activity-related controls that can be affected by account or admin settings.

officialSources.title: Show changes that were made in a workbook
evidence: Microsoft documents Excel Show Changes for who changed what, where and when, with prior cell values.
Microsoft documents that Show Changes can filter by sheet, range, cell, and bulk edits.

officialSources.title: View previous versions of Office files
evidence: Microsoft documents previous-version browsing for Office files stored in OneDrive or SharePoint.
Microsoft documents version comparison and restoration.

officialSources.title: Insert comments and notes in Excel
evidence: Microsoft documents threaded comments and notes in Excel.
Microsoft documents mentions in comments to send email notifications and assign tasks.

officialSources.title: Track changes in a shared workbook
evidence: Microsoft documents legacy shared-workbook change tracking and change-history worksheets.
Microsoft documents that many Excel features are unsupported in shared workbooks.

limitations: This artifact captures current official public documentation, not a live audit export from Google Sheets or Microsoft Excel tenants.
The comparison proves direct external auditability evidence exists; it does not claim bilig is 10x better across auditability or every objective category.

dimensions.title: Version And Cell Change History
biligEvidence: The auditability scorecard exercises deterministic workbook history derivation across own changes, collaborator changes, revert linkage, and redo linkage.
The reliability scorecard separately proves mutation-journal and authoritative replay behavior for pending, submitted, failed, and rebased operations.
googleSheetsEvidence: Google documents version history, named versions, restoration, and cell edit history.
Google documents Activity stream access from the Sheets Manage data help surface.
microsoftExcelEvidence: Microsoft documents Excel Show Changes with actor, location, time, and prior value details.
Microsoft documents previous Office file versions for OneDrive or SharePoint files.
dimensions.comparisonResult: bilig has executable history-state and mutation-journal controls; Sheets and Excel expose documented version and cell-change history surfaces for supported storage modes.
residualLimitations: bilig still needs a user-facing exportable audit log artifact before claiming product-wide auditability dominance.
The external incumbent comparison is documentation-based and does not test retention under tenant-specific policy.

dimensions.title: Review Apply Revert Workflows
biligEvidence: The auditability scorecard executes workbook-agent preview parity, authoritative preview mismatch fails-closed behavior, and captured undo bundles.
The headed-browser auditability control verifies a remote-sync change review and revert flow exists in the UI test suite.
googleSheetsEvidence: Google documents previous-version restoration for Sheets.
Google documents named versions and collaboration surfaces including comments and notifications.
microsoftExcelEvidence: Microsoft documents prior-version restore for Office files.
Microsoft documents Excel Show Changes as a review surface for changed workbook cells.
dimensions.comparisonResult: bilig's AI-agent workflow has executable preview/apply/revert gates; Sheets and Excel focus on version restoration and change review rather than agent-command preview parity.
residualLimitations: bilig needs broader headed-browser evidence across more workbook commands before this becomes a blanket auditability win.
Google and Microsoft version restore can cover whole-file revisions that bilig's current scorecard does not fully compare.

dimensions.title: Comment Task Accountability
biligEvidence: bilig's checked auditability evidence covers workbook-agent command provenance and revertable mutation history.
bilig does not yet have a generated direct comparison for comment threads, task assignment, or viewer activity.
googleSheetsEvidence: Google documents comments, task assignment, email notifications, and an Activity dashboard for Sheets.
Google documents that activity visibility can depend on account and admin settings.
microsoftExcelEvidence: Microsoft documents threaded comments, notes, mentions, email notifications, and task assignment in Excel.
Microsoft documents comment surfaces separately from Show Changes and version history.
dimensions.comparisonResult: bilig has strong command-level audit rails, while Sheets and Excel have broader documented comment/task accountability surfaces.
residualLimitations: This dimension remains a product gap for overall auditability dominance.
Future bilig work should add first-class comment/task audit evidence or explicitly scope auditability around workbook operations.

dimensions.title: History Retention And Limitations
biligEvidence: The auditability scorecard requires generated uncovered controls to be explicit instead of silently assuming parity.
The current bilig evidence is limited to executable preview, apply, undo, revert, and headed-browser change review checks.
googleSheetsEvidence: Google documents that cell edit history does not show some changes, such as row or column changes, format changes, formulas, and hidden rows.
Google documents that version history may merge changes to save storage space.
microsoftExcelEvidence: Microsoft documents that Show Changes displays changes for a limited recent history window.
Microsoft documents feature restrictions for legacy shared-workbook change tracking.
dimensions.comparisonResult: bilig's generated scorecard makes current evidence boundaries explicit; official Sheets and Excel docs also disclose history and feature limitations.
residualLimitations: bilig still needs a retention policy and audit-log compaction strategy before claiming superior long-term auditability.
The official documentation comparison is not a forensic audit of all incumbent history edge cases.
